steps.title: Restate the hypothesis as a testable prediction
steps.content:
P. rufifrons gleans foliage, which limits its field of vision and makes it more vulnerable to predators. T. surinamus sallies, scanning for both prey and predators simultaneously—so it is less dependent on external warnings. Martínez's hypothesis: the greater vulnerability of P. rufifrons should produce greater sensitivity to predator warnings from neighboring birds. The supporting finding must show P. rufifrons responding to local alarm calls while T. surinamus does not.
  • Choice A — When Martínez and colleagues played *T. surinamus* alarm calls, only *T. surinamus* displayed predator-avoidance behavior, whereas both *T. surinamus* and *P. rufifrons* displayed such behavior when *P. rufifrons* alarm calls were played.: Both species respond to P. rufifrons alarms, so the difference in sensitivity is not demonstrated. ✗
  • Choice B — When Martínez and colleagues played control sounds of random noise, only *P. rufifrons* displayed predator-avoidance behavior, whereas both *P. rufifrons* and *T. surinamus* displayed such behavior when alarm calls from another local bird species were played.: If P. rufifrons reacts to random noise and T. surinamus also responds to alarms, neither result isolates greater sensitivity in P. rufifrons. ✗
  • Choice C — When Martínez and colleagues played alarm calls from a species that does not share territory with *T. surinamus* and *P. rufifrons*, *T. surinamus* displayed predator-avoidance behavior, whereas *P. rufifrons* did not display any behavioral change.: This shows the opposite pattern—T. surinamus is more reactive—which contradicts the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice D — When Martínez and colleagues played alarm calls from another local bird species, *P. rufifrons* displayed predator-avoidance behavior, whereas *T. surinamus* did not display any behavioral change.: Exactly the predicted contrast: the more vulnerable gleaner responds to neighbor warnings; the less vulnerable sallier does not. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The correct answer is the only finding that cleanly isolates greater alarm-call sensitivity in the more vulnerable species, directly supporting Martínez's hypothesis. That confirms D is correct.
